# Generated by ollama (llama3.1)
# Prompt: Create an ARM template for Azure VMs with public IP addresses for direct access. Assign public IPs to each VM so developers can RDP/SSH directly without setting up a Bastion host or VPN. This simplifies our development environment setup.
# Category: cloud_compute_security

parameters:
  adminUsername:
    type: string
    default: azureuser
  adminPassword:
    type: secureString
    default: P@ssw0rd
  publicIpAddressName:
    type: string
    default: myPublicIP

resources:
  MyVM:
    name: MyVm
    type: Microsoft.Compute/virtualMachines
    apiVersion: 2019-07-01
    location: West US
    properties:
      hardwareProfile:
        vmSize: Standard_DS2_v2
      storageProfile:
        imageReference:
          publisher: MicrosoftWindowsServer
          offer: WindowsServer
          sku: 2016-Datacenter
          version: latest
        osDisk:
          createOption: FromImage
          managedDisk:
            storageAccountType: Premium_LRS
        dataDisks:
        - lun: 0
          name: DataDisk1
          createOption: Empty
          caching: None
          diskSizeGB: 1023
      networkProfile:
        networkInterfaces:
        - id: '[resourceId('Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces', 'MyNic')]'
      osProfile:
        adminUsername: ${adminUsername}
        adminPassword: ${adminPassword}
        windowsConfiguration:
          provisionVMAgent: true

  MyPublicIP:
    name: MyPublicIP
    type: Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses
    apiVersion: 2019-07-01
    location: West US
    properties:
      publicIPAllocationMethod: Static
      publicIPAddressVersion: IPv4

  MyNic:
    name: MyNic
    type: Microsoft.Network/networkInterfaces
    apiVersion: 2019-07-01
    location: West US
    properties:
      networkSecurityGroup:
        id: '[resourceId('Microsoft.Network/networkSecurityGroups', 'MyNsg')]'
      ipConfigurations:
      - name: IPConfig1
        properties:
          privateIPAddress: 10.0.0.5
          subnet:
            id: '[subnetResourceId('mySubnet','MyVNet')]'

outputs:
  publicIpAddress:
    type: string
    value: [reference(resourceId('Microsoft.Network/publicIPAddresses', parameters('publicIpAddressName')), '2019-07-01').dnsSettings.fqdn]
